items.title: Spam filter with wrong priors
items.scenario: A company estimates 1% of mail is spam (P) but a legacy filter assumes 20% spam (Q). Each mis-calibrated prediction adds expected log-ratio cost when real traffic follows P.
items.explanation: D_KL(P || Q) quantifies the average extra surprise from using Q's spam rate instead of P's. Even small prior mismatches accumulate across millions of messages.

items.title: Language model fine-tuning with a KL cap
items.scenario: An RLHF pipeline fine-tunes policy π_new on reward while penalizing D_KL(π_new || π_ref) against a frozen reference model π_ref to avoid catastrophic drift from safe behavior.
items.explanation: The KL penalty keeps new token distributions close to the reference where the reference had mass, preventing the model from collapsing into high-reward but nonsensical outputs.

items.title: Teacher–student logits in distillation
items.scenario: A large teacher outputs softmax probabilities over 50K vocabulary tokens; a smaller student is trained to match those probabilities on unlabeled text using KL between temperature-scaled distributions.
items.explanation: KL on softened distributions transfers dark knowledge (relative probabilities of wrong classes), not just the argmax label — something plain cross-entropy on hard labels misses.
